label: dexamethasone treatment for hospitalized COVID-19
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Systemic glucocorticoid therapy (dexamethasone 6 mg once daily for up to 10 days) for patients hospitalized with COVID-19.

label: Protective mechanical ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Mechanical ventilation strategy designed to minimize alveolar injury from cyclic collapse/reopening and overdistention, involving higher PEEP, lower tidal volumes, and pressure-limited modes.

label: Conservative fluid management strategy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Conservative fluid management strategy applied for 7 days in patients with acute lung injury.

label: Anakinra early treatment of COVID-19
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Early treatment with anakinra, an IL-1α/β inhibitor, in COVID-19 patients at risk of progressing to respiratory failure as identified by elevated plasma suPAR levels.

label: Delirium duration
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Duration of delirium during critical illness, associated with long-term cognitive and executive function impairment.

label: Higher PEEP levels
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Mechanical ventilation with higher positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) levels in patients with acute lung injury and ARDS.

label: lung ultrasound for acute respiratory failure diagnosis
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Systematic lung ultrasound assessment including artifacts, lung sliding, consolidation, and pleural effusion to differentiate causes of acute respiratory failure.
original_label: lung ultrasound for acute respiratory failure diagnosis

label: Lung-protective ventilation strategy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation strategy to minimize alveolar recruitment/derecruitment and overdistention, thereby reducing ventilation-induced pulmonary and systemic cytokine response.

label: once-daily spontaneous breathing trial
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilator weaning using a single daily trial of spontaneous breathing, rather than multiple or continuous trials.

label: ARDS subphenotype identification
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Latent class analysis to identify distinct subphenotypes within acute respiratory distress syndrome based on clinical and biological data.

label: Early ICU mobility therapy protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: A protocol using an ICU mobility team to initiate physical therapy within 48 hours of mechanical ventilation for acute respiratory failure.

label: prone positioning
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Placing patients in a prone (face down) position for six or more hours daily for 10 days.

label: Treatment protocol for acute chest syndrome in sickle cell disease
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Treatment protocol including matched transfusions, bronchodilators, and bronchoscopy for acute chest syndrome in sickle cell disease.
original_label: Treatment protocol for acute chest syndrome in sickle cell disease

label: non-invasive ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Non-invasive ventilation to improve survival and quality of life in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

label: Restrictive red blood cell transfusion strategy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Maintain hematocrit ≥24% in patients undergoing cardiac surgery with cardiopulmonary bypass.
original_label: Restrictive red blood cell transfusion strategy

label: epidural anaesthesia and analgesia
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Use of epidural anaesthesia intraoperatively and epidural analgesia postoperatively for major abdominal surgery.

label: Noninvasive positive-pressure ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Noninvasive ventilation delivered through a face mask for patients with acute respiratory failure.

label: growth hormone administration
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Administration of growth hormone to critically ill adults hospitalized for prolonged periods.

label: prolonged methylprednisolone therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Prolonged administration of methylprednisolone (initial dose 2 mg/kg/day, duration 32 days) for unresolving ARDS.

label: pulmonary-artery-catheter-guided-hemodynamic-management
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Use of pulmonary artery catheter to guide hemodynamic management in patients with acute lung injury.

label: respiratory changes in inferior vena cava diameter
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Measuring respiratory changes in inferior vena cava diameter to predict fluid responsiveness in septic patients with acute lung injury.
original_label: respiratory changes in inferior vena cava diameter

label: trophic enteral feeding for first 6 days
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Trophic enteral feeding providing 400 kcal/day for the first 6 days in patients with acute lung injury requiring mechanical ventilation.
original_label: trophic enteral feeding for first 6 days

label: Tocilizumab for COVID-19 pneumonia
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Tocilizumab 8 mg/kg IV on day 1 and day 3 if clinically indicated, for treatment of moderate to severe COVID-19 pneumonia.

label: Enteral feeding with EPA+GLA and antioxidants
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Enteral feeding with a combination of eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA), gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), and antioxidants to reduce pulmonary inflammation and improve oxygenation in patients with acute respiratory distress syndrome.
normalized_label: Enteral nutrition with omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants
original_label: Enteral feeding with EPA+GLA and antioxidants

label: status asthmaticus inflammatory profile
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Upregulation of proinflammatory cytokines IL-1, IL-6, and TNF-alpha in bronchoalveolar lavage of patients with status asthmaticus, despite concomitant increase in antiinflammatory mediators.

label: Lower oxygenation target (Pao2 60 mmHg)
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
definition: Oxygen therapy targeting a partial pressure of arterial oxygen (Pao2) of 60 mmHg in patients with acute hypoxemic respiratory failure.

label: Global strength and handgrip dynamometry assessment
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Comprehensive assessment of global muscle strength and handgrip dynamometry to diagnose ICU-acquired paresis and predict mortality in critically ill patients.
original_label: Global strength and handgrip dynamometry assessment

label: large tidal volume ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation with tidal volumes larger than 6 mL/kg predicted body weight, a proposed risk factor for ventilator-associated lung injury and ARDS.

label: Injurious ventilation strategy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation strategy that leads to increased epithelial cell apoptosis in the kidney and small intestine, contributing to organ dysfunction in ARDS.

label: early use of pulmonary artery catheter
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Early use of pulmonary artery catheter in patients with shock and acute respiratory distress syndrome.
original_label: early use of pulmonary artery catheter

label: recombinant human angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (rhACE2)
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Recombinant human angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (rhACE2, GSK2586881) to replete ACE2 and modulate renin-angiotensin system signaling in acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: aerosolized synthetic surfactant
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Continuous administration of aerosolized synthetic surfactant (13.5 mg/mL dipalmitoylphosphatidylcholine) for treatment of sepsis-induced ARDS.

label: Peak cough flow threshold for extubation/decannulation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ability to generate peak cough flow of at least 160 L/min predicts successful extubation and tracheostomy decannulation in patients with neuromuscular ventilatory insufficiency.
original_label: Peak cough flow threshold for extubation/decannulation

label: haloperidol for delirium in critical illness
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intravenous haloperidol with maximum daily dose of 20 mg for treatment of delirium in critically ill patients.
original_label: haloperidol for delirium in critical illness

label: inflammasome pathway regulation
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: The inflammasome pathway and its downstream cytokines play critical roles in ARDS development.

label: serial blood lactate measurements
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Serial monitoring of blood lactate levels to assess patient response to therapy and guide management of acutely ill patients.

label: Superior vena caval collapsibility measurement
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Measurement of superior vena caval collapsibility as an index of fluid responsiveness in septic shock patients on mechanical ventilation.

label: Furosemide with albumin therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Combination of furosemide diuretic and albumin in hypoproteinemic patients with acute lung injury or ARDS.

label: Supplemental oxygen during perioperative period
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Administration of supplemental oxygen (FiO2 > 0.21) during the perioperative period.

label: Negative cumulative fluid balance in acute lung injury
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Negative cumulative fluid balance on day 4 of acute lung injury, associated with reduced hospital mortality, more ventilator-free days, and more ICU-free days.
original_label: Negative cumulative fluid balance in acute lung injury

label: partial liquid ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Perflubron administration into trachea until dependent lung zone filled, followed by gas ventilation of perflubron-filled lung.

label: Sepsis molecular phenotyping
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
definition: Derivation of sepsis molecular phenotypes (hypoinflammatory, hyperinflammatory) using latent class analysis of clinical data and biomarkers. Evaluation of concordance with ARDS phenotypes and implications for personalized management.

label: cuff-leak test
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Diagnostic test to detect upper airway obstruction by measuring air leak around an endotracheal tube cuff.

label: elevated plasma sRAGE
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Elevated plasma levels of soluble receptor for advanced glycation end products (sRAGE) associated with nonfocal CT-based ARDS lung imaging pattern.
normalized_label: soluble receptor for advanced glycation end products

label: Protocol-directed sedation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Nursing-implemented protocol for sedation administration during mechanical ventilation

label: pressure- and volume-limited ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation strategy that limits peak airway pressure to ≤30 cmH2O and tidal volume to ≤8 mL/kg in patients at high risk for ARDS.

label: high-frequency oscillatory ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Use of high-frequency oscillatory ventilation for moderate-to-severe ARDS.

label: non-invasive positive pressure ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Non-invasive positive pressure ventilation to manage respiratory failure in COPD exacerbations.

label: Methylprednisolone infusion
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Methylprednisolone infusion at 1 mg/kg/day for up to 28 days in early severe ARDS, to down-regulate systemic inflammation and improve pulmonary and extrapulmonary organ dysfunction.

label: Physiotherapy for critically ill adults
subset:LABEL: POI_population
definition: Physiotherapy targeting deconditioning, impaired airway clearance, atelectasis, intubation avoidance, and weaning failure in critically ill adult patients.

label: Tocilizumab
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intravenous tocilizumab 8mg/kg up to 800mg, administered twice within 12 hours, for treatment of COVID-19 pneumonia.

label: fluid challenge
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Fluid challenge with either 1,000 ml crystalloids or 500 ml colloids to assess fluid responsiveness.

label: Lymphopenia on admission
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Low lymphocyte count on admission as a marker of severe COVID-19 disease course.

label: noninvasive pressure support ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Extubation and application of noninvasive pressure support ventilation by face mask, used as an alternative to invasive pressure support ventilation for weaning patients with COPD and acute hypercapnic respiratory failure.

label: Airway pressure release ventilation with spontaneous breathing
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation strategy using APRV mode with continuous spontaneous breathing, enabled by infusion of sedatives (Ramsay score 3).
original_label: Airway pressure release ventilation with spontaneous breathing

label: Lung expansion interventions
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Incentive spirometry, deep breathing exercises, and continuous positive airway pressure to expand and recruit atelectatic lung regions after noncardiothoracic surgery.

label: Noninvasive ventilation delivered by helmet
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Noninvasive ventilation delivered via a transparent helmet covering the entire head of the patient with a rubber collar neck seal, used for patients with acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: Enteral n-3 fatty acid, gamma-linolenic acid, and antioxidant supplementation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Twice-daily enteral supplementation of n-3 fatty acids, gamma-linolenic acid, and antioxidants for patients with acute lung injury.
normalized_label: Enteral nutritional supplementation with omega-3 fatty acids, gamma-linolenic acid, and antioxidants
original_label: Enteral n-3 fatty acid, gamma-linolenic acid, and antioxidant supplementation

label: introduction of medical emergency team
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intensive care unit-based medical emergency team to evaluate and treat hospitalized patients at risk of adverse outcomes.

label: Physical rehabilitation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Physical rehabilitation for patients with intensive care unit-acquired weakness (ICUAW)

label: ICU intubation bundle management protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
definition: Multicomponent protocol including preoxygenation, two operators, rapid sequence induction, cricoid pressure, capnography, protective ventilation, fluid loading, early sedation, and vasopressors as needed.

label: Intraoperative protective ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intraoperative ventilation strategy targeting PEEP ≥5 cmH2O, tidal volume <10 mL/kg PBW, and plateau pressure <30 cmH2O.

label: Scheduled neuromuscular blockade
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Continuous infusion of neuromuscular-blocking agents with scheduled eye care including lubricating drops/gel and eyelid closure.

label: Preoperative physiologic assessment for lung cancer resection
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Structured preoperative evaluation of lung cancer patients prior to resection surgery, including cardiovascular assessment, spirometry, DLCO, estimation of postop pulmonary reserve, and cardiopulmonary exercise testing.
original_label: Preoperative physiologic assessment for lung cancer resection

label: TAK-242
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Small-molecule inhibitor of Toll-like receptor-4-mediated signaling for the treatment of severe sepsis.

label: inhaled nitric oxide
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Administration of inhaled nitric oxide as a treatment for acute lung injury or acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: Nasal positive-pressure ventilation plus oxygen
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Combination of nasal positive-pressure ventilation and long-term oxygen therapy for treatment of stable hypercapnic COPD.
normalized_label: Nasal Positive Pressure Ventilation with Oxygen Therapy

label: inspiratory pressure augmentation during spontaneous breathing trials
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Use of inspiratory pressure augmentation during spontaneous breathing trials to improve ventilator liberation outcomes.
original_label: inspiratory pressure augmentation during spontaneous breathing trials

label: Tofacitinib treatment
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Tofacitinib 10 mg PO BID for up to 14 days or hospital discharge in patients hospitalized with COVID-19 pneumonia.

label: Multidisciplinary ventilator management protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilator management protocol implemented by respiratory care practitioners and nurses.

label: PEEP titration guided by esophageal pressure in ARDS
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Titrating PEEP using esophageal pressure to offset pleural pressure and attenuate alveolar injury in ARDS.
original_label: PEEP titration guided by esophageal pressure in ARDS

label: Recombinant surfactant protein C-based surfactant
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Recombinant surfactant protein C-based surfactant administered intratracheally up to 4 times within 24 hours in patients with ARDS.

label: Implementation of ICU nutrition management protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Implementation of a guideline-based protocol to increase enteral nutrition use and enable earlier feeding initiation in critically ill patients.
original_label: Implementation of ICU nutrition management protocol

label: ACE I/D polymorphism
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: ACE insertion/deletion polymorphism that regulates ACE activity and is associated with susceptibility and outcome in ARDS.

label: Predictors of extubation failure
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
definition: Elevated rapid shallow breathing index, positive fluid balance 24h prior, and pneumonia as reason for intubation are associated with increased risk of extubation failure in ventilated ICU patients.

label: 80% perioperative oxygen
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Administration of 80% oxygen during and for 2 hours after abdominal surgery.

label: Computer-driven weaning protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Automated protocol including gradual reduction in pressure support, automatic performance of spontaneous breathing trials, and feedback messaging on SBT success.

label: temporal trend of improved ALI mortality
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
definition: Unspecified improvements in critical care over 1996-2005 associated with declining ALI mortality at ARDS Network centers, beyond the benefits of low tidal volume ventilation.
original_label: temporal trend of improved ALI mortality

label: diaphragm ultrasonography
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ultrasound-based technique to measure diaphragm excursion, thickness, and thickening fraction, allowing detection of diaphragmatic dysfunction in critically ill patients.

label: standardized rehabilitation therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Daily therapy consisting of passive range of motion, physical therapy, and progressive resistance exercise until hospital discharge, provided to patients with acute respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation.

label: noninvasive measurement of indocyanine green plasma disappearance rate
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Noninvasive transcutaneous sensor measurement of indocyanine green plasma disappearance rate, compared to invasive aortic fiberoptic reference.
original_label: noninvasive measurement of indocyanine green plasma disappearance rate

label: bovine surfactant therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Endotracheal instillation of bovine surfactant in patients with acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: intensive insulin therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intensive insulin therapy to maintain euglycemia in critically ill patients, preventing critical illness polyneuropathy/myopathy and reducing duration of mechanical ventilation.

label: corticosteroids
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Corticosteroids administered for prevention and treatment of acute respiratory distress syndrome in critically ill adults.

label: recombinant human soluble thrombomodulin
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Recombinant human soluble thrombomodulin administered intravenously at 0.06 mg/kg/day (max 6 mg/day) for 6 days for treatment of sepsis-associated coagulopathy.

label: Bilevel positive airway pressure (BPV) for acute asthmatic attack
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Applying bilevel positive airway pressure (BPV) in addition to conventional asthma treatment for patients with a severe asthmatic attack.
original_label: Bilevel positive airway pressure (BPV) for acute asthmatic attack

label: ventilatory ratio
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Bedside index of impaired ventilation calculated as [minute ventilation (ml/min) × PaCO2 (mm Hg)]/(predicted body weight × 100 × 37.5).

label: Sivelestat (ONO-5046, Elaspol)
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Small molecular weight inhibitor of neutrophil elastase.

label: Work Of Breathing Reduction Nppv
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
normalized_label: Work of Breathing Reduction by Non-invasive Positive Pressure Ventilation

label: De Novo Acute Respiratory Failure Pathogenesis
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
original_label: De Novo Acute Respiratory Failure Pathogenesis

label: Acute On Chronic Respiratory Failure Pathogenesis
subset:LABEL: POI_disease
original_label: Acute On Chronic Respiratory Failure Pathogenesis

label: respiratory muscle aid protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Home-based protocol using noninvasive ventilation and assisted coughing to prevent or reverse oxyhemoglobin desaturation and respiratory distress episodes in neuromuscular disease patients.

label: Reconceptualization of critical illness
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Deemphasizing syndromes, focus on underlying biological changes that underpin critical illness states and may be amenable to treatment.

label: Intensive PT program
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intensive physical therapy program for up to 4 weeks in patients with acute respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation.

label: increased body mass index
subset:LABEL: POI_population
definition: Higher body mass index

label: Intravenous N-acetylcysteine (NAC)
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Intravenous administration of N-acetylcysteine at 40 mg/kg/day for 3 days to enhance recovery from acute lung injury.

label: Assessment for patient-ventilator trigger asynchrony
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Observation and monitoring of patient-ventilator interactions to identify and attempt to mitigate trigger asynchrony in ventilator-dependent patients.

label: optoelectronic plethysmography
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Non-invasive technique to measure regional chest wall volume changes in intensive care patients.

label: Tracheostomy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Tracheostomy procedure in ventilator-dependent patients to reduce the resistive work of breathing and respiratory muscle load.

label: patient-directed music
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Self-initiated patient music listening tailored by a music therapist during mechanical ventilation.

label: respiratory therapist-driven weaning protocol
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Protocol-driven weaning approach implemented by respiratory therapists, including daily screening, spontaneous breathing trials, and physician prompting.

label: Education program for VAP prevention
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Multifaceted education program for ICU staff including self-study module, in-service training, and visual aids to highlight best practices for preventing ventilator-associated pneumonia.
normalized_label: Education program for ventilator-associated pneumonia prevention

label: Fiberoptic bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Fiberoptic bronchoscopy combined with bronchoalveolar lavage for diagnosis of acute respiratory failure in hematology and oncology patients.

label: arterial hyperoxia
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Elevated arterial oxygen levels during critical illness.

label: incentive spirometry
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Breathing exercise using a device that provides feedback to the patient on inspiratory flow and volume

label: Immune-modulating diets (IMDs)
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Enteral nutrition formulas supplemented with immune-modulating components like arginine, glutamine, and/or fish oil.

label: inappropriately low EPO levels in critical illness
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Blunted erythropoietin response contributes to the development of anemia in critically ill patients.
original_label: inappropriately low EPO levels in critical illness

label: Candida respiratory colonization
subset:LABEL: POI_context
definition: Presence of Candida species in respiratory samples of critically ill, mechanically ventilated patients.

label: early activity protocol in respiratory ICU
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Prospective application of early activity protocol to respiratory failure patients transferred to a dedicated respiratory ICU.
original_label: early activity protocol in respiratory ICU

label: Recruitment maneuver
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Application of 35-40 cmH2O CPAP for 30 seconds to recruit collapsed alveoli in ARDS patients ventilated with high PEEP.

label: nitric oxide inhalation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Inhalation of nitric oxide, a selective pulmonary vasodilator, for the treatment of acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: hypercapnic acidosis
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Elevated PaCO2 and low pH, proposed to have a protective effect against ventilator-associated lung injury.

label: Heterogeneity of treatment effect awareness in critical care RCTs
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Awareness that patients in critical care RCTs have wide variability in risk and thus in the absolute benefit they can derive from a given therapy.
original_label: Heterogeneity of treatment effect awareness in critical care RCTs

label: Resuscitation guided by fluid responsiveness assessment
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Resuscitation of septic shock guided by assessment of fluid responsiveness using passive leg raise to determine fluid bolus or vasopressor administration.
original_label: Resuscitation guided by fluid responsiveness assessment

label: postoperative respiratory failure risk calculator
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Predictive model using preoperative factors to estimate risk of postoperative respiratory failure requiring prolonged mechanical ventilation or unplanned intubation.

label: Prophylactic nasal continuous positive airway pressure (nCPAP) following cardiac surgery
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Prophylactic nasal continuous positive airway pressure at 10 cmH2O for at least 6 hours following cardiac surgery to prevent postoperative pulmonary complications.
original_label: Prophylactic nasal continuous positive airway pressure (nCPAP) following cardiac surgery

label: Sarilumab
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Monoclonal antibody interleukin-6 receptor inhibitor

label: individualised open-lung approach during one-lung ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Individualised ventilation strategy during one-lung ventilation combining alveolar recruitment manoeuvre to 40 cmH2O with PEEP titrated to best respiratory system compliance plus postop HFNO.
original_label: individualised open-lung approach during one-lung ventilation

label: Respiratory physiotherapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Respiratory physiotherapy techniques including deep breathing, directed cough, and postural drainage to prevent pulmonary complications after abdominal surgery.

label: therapeutic hypercapnia
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Supplemental carbon dioxide administration to critically ill patients to raise CO2 concentrations and promote the protective effects of hypercapnia while reducing the injurious effects of hypocapnia.

label: recombinant factor VIIa
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Recombinant factor VIIa, a hemostatic agent approved for hemophilia, increasingly used for off-label indications.

label: time-cycled inspiratory pressure support ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Time-cycled expiratory trigger during noninvasive inspiratory pressure support ventilation to improve patient-ventilator synchronization in the presence of mask air leaks.

label: Time Cycled Vs Flow Cycled Expiratory Trigger
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
original_label: Time Cycled Vs Flow Cycled Expiratory Trigger

label: Inhaled sevoflurane
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Inhaled sevoflurane for sedation in acute respiratory distress syndrome.

label: Limiting plateau pressure during mechanical ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
definition: Maintaining end-inspiratory plateau pressure (Pplat) below 35 cmH2O to prevent barotrauma in ARDS patients.
original_label: Limiting plateau pressure during mechanical ventilation

label: neurally adjusted ventilatory assist (NAVA)
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Ventilation mode where assistance is provided in proportion to diaphragm electrical activity.

label: pulmonary vascular dysfunction
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Elevation in pulmonary artery pressure and pulmonary vascular resistance in the setting of acute lung injury.

label: cellular repair mechanisms in ventilator-associated lung injury
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
definition: Alveolar epithelial and endothelial cells possess repair and remodeling mechanisms that can protect against mechanical stretch-induced injury and inflammation in ventilator-associated lung injury.
original_label: cellular repair mechanisms in ventilator-associated lung injury

label: E-PRE-DELIRIC model
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Prediction model for delirium risk at ICU admission based on 9 factors: age, history of cognitive impairment, alcohol abuse, blood urea nitrogen, admission category, urgent admission, mean arterial pressure, corticosteroid use, and respiratory failure.

label: Activated protein C
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Activated protein C administered at 24 microg/kg/h for 96 h for the treatment of acute lung injury.

label: prevention of unplanned extubation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Measures to prevent unplanned extubation, including strong fixation of the tracheal tube, adequate sedation of agitated patients, attention to orotracheally intubated patients, and daily reassessment of weaning readiness.

label: low-dose intravenous GM-CSF therapy
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Low-dose (3 mcg/kg) intravenous recombinant human granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF) administered daily for 5 days in patients with severe sepsis and respiratory dysfunction.

label: Gm Csf Stimulated Macrophage Effector Function
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
original_label: Gm Csf Stimulated Macrophage Effector Function

label: Gm Csf Regulation Of Pulmonary Surfactant
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
normalized_label: Regulation of pulmonary surfactant by granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor
original_label: Gm Csf Regulation Of Pulmonary Surfactant

label: Extravascular lung water measurement
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Transpulmonary thermodilution used to measure extravascular lung water in patients with severe sepsis.

label: noninvasive positive pressure ventilation for obesity hypoventilation syndrome
subset:LABEL: POI_population
original_label: noninvasive positive pressure ventilation for obesity hypoventilation syndrome

label: BREATHE criteria for breath stacking dyssynchrony
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Novel, objective criteria for quantifying breath stacking dyssynchrony in ARDS, evaluating 5 domains: ventilator cycling, interval expiratory volume, cumulative inspiratory volume, expiratory time, and inspiratory time.
original_label: BREATHE criteria for breath stacking dyssynchrony

label: Pressure limitation with permissive hypercapnia
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
definition: Approach to minimize alveolar overdistention through either pressure or volume limitation, while accepting the potential consequences of respiratory acidosis.

label: No change in nonpulmonary organ failures
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: No change in nonpulmonary organ failures

label: Reduced pulmonary and systemic cytokine response
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: Reduced pulmonary and systemic cytokine response

label: Dependent lung atelectasis with one-lung ventilation
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
original_label: Dependent lung atelectasis with one-lung ventilation

subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: Higher mortality, fewer ventilator-free days, fewer organ failure-free days

label: Reduced ICU length of stay
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
normalized_label: Intensive care unit length of stay

label: Improved quality of life in ALS
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: Improved quality of life in ALS

label: No difference in major adverse outcomes
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: No difference in major adverse outcomes

subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
normalized_label: Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve
original_label: External validation of RESP score AUC

label: No impact on hospital length of stay
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: No impact on hospital length of stay

label: Epithelial cell apoptosis in small intestine
subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
original_label: Epithelial cell apoptosis in small intestine

label: Ziprasidone for delirium in critical illness
subset:LABEL: POI_intervention
original_label: Ziprasidone for delirium in critical illness

label: Reduced worse clinical status on WHO-CPS at day 28
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: Reduced worse clinical status on WHO-CPS at day 28

subset:LABEL: POI_mechanism
original_label: SVC collapse due to ventricular underfilling

label: Increased mortality in acute kidney injury
subset:LABEL: POI_outcome
original_label: Increased mortality in acute kidney injury
